description_long: This object is a classic lever-action rifle, featuring a long, blued steel barrel and a polished wooden stock with a rich, reddish-brown finish. The receiver and lever mechanism are metallic, with the lever loop positioned just behind the trigger guard, characteristic of traditional lever-action designs. The stock is fixed and smoothly contoured, providing a comfortable shoulder rest, while the fore-end is also wooden and matches the stock in color and finish. The barrel is slender and extends well beyond the fore-end, ending in a simple muzzle with no visible attachments. Iron sights are mounted along the top of the barrel, with a rear notch and a front post for aiming. The overall appearance is that of a well-maintained, vintage hunting or sporting rifle, likely used for target shooting or small to medium game hunting.
description: A classic lever-action rifle with a long blued barrel, polished wooden stock, and iron sights. The design is traditional, with a metallic lever and matching wooden fore-end.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, highlighting the wooden stock, lever mechanism, and long barrel.
description_view_1: Front-on view showing the narrow profile of the barrel and the alignment of the iron sights.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the stock, lever, and barrel from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear-on view showing the butt of the stock and the slim profile of the rifle's length.
